L1.area: REST API endpoint routing and access control configuration

L2.description: An authentication bypass vulnerability exists where multiple API endpoints have misconfigured access control annotations that include a permissive public access level alongside restricted group requirements. Because the access control system treats any matching rule as sufficient, the public access level effectively overrides the restricted requirement, allowing unauthenticated users to invoke privileged operations including site backup creation and download. The predictable timestamp-based naming of generated backup archives makes it trivial for an attacker to guess and retrieve them.

L3.area: API controller route annotation access control lists for archive management, user administration, and content modification endpoints
L3.description: An authentication bypass vulnerability exists where multiple API endpoints have misconfigured access control annotations that include a permissive public access level alongside admin-only or authenticated-user-only group requirements. Because the access control system treats any matching ACL entry as sufficient, the public entry effectively overrides the restricted requirement. This allows unauthenticated users to perform privileged operations such as creating and downloading full site backups, managing user accounts and groups, and modifying content. The predictable timestamp-based naming of generated backup archives makes it trivial for an attacker to guess and retrieve them without authentication.

ground_truth.reason: Multiple API route annotations incorrectly include "public" in their ACL options alongside restricted group requirements, allowing unauthenticated users to access admin-only and authenticated-only endpoints including site backup creation and download.
